skill: Linear inequalities in one or two variables
stem: A number x is at most 5 less than 4 times the value of y. If the value of y is -3, what is the greatest possible value of x?
rationale: The statement gives x ≤ 4y - 5. When y = -3, 4y - 5 = -12 - 5 = -17. So x ≤ -17 and the greatest value of x is -17.
